title: Otophora multijuga
taxonomy: Otophora multijuga belongs to the family of Apocynaceae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This classification places Otophora multijuga within the order Gentianales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the gentian, loganiaceae, and rubiaceae families. The Apocynaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Otophora multijuga being part of the subfamily Apocyonoideae. This subfamily is characterized by its unique combination of morphological and anatomical features. Otophora multijuga's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Apocynaceae family.
uses: Otophora multijuga has been reported to have medicinal uses, primarily in traditional African medicine. Its leaves and roots are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In some cultures, it is also used as a treatment for malaria and other infectious diseases. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
